title: AWS Static Hosting Architecture
summary: This website is intentionally built as a static, data-driven React app to demonstrate practical AWS hosting, caching, and deployment patterns.
requestFlow: CloudFront serves cached assets over HTTPS
CloudFront retrieves origin files from the private S3 bucket when needed
The React app loads JSON files from /data and renders the portfolio

components.name: S3
components.purpose: Stores the built Vite app and runtime JSON content files.

components.name: CloudFront
components.purpose: Provides CDN caching, HTTPS entry point, and SPA routing behavior in front of the private S3 origin.

components.name: Route53
components.purpose: Maps zhenwei.dev to the CloudFront distribution.

components.name: ACM
components.purpose: Provides the TLS certificate for HTTPS.

components.name: GitHub Actions
components.purpose: Runs manual deployment workflows for either the full app build or data-only updates.

components.name: OIDC + IAM
components.purpose: Allows GitHub Actions to deploy without storing long-lived AWS access keys.

pipelineDemo.title: Current Deployment Flow
steps: Update app code or JSON content in the repository
Run the GitHub Actions workflow manually with deploy_type set to app or data
GitHub Actions assumes the AWS deploy role using OIDC
The workflow syncs build output or JSON data to S3 with cache-control rules by content type
CloudFront cache is invalidated for either all paths or only /data/*
The live website serves the updated application or content
